Paddy.irrigate: 2–5cm standing water during tillering & panicle initiation. Drain 1 week before harvest. Skip if rainfall >20mm/day.
Paddy.fertilize: NPK 90:45:45 kg/ha. Basal: DAP+MOP before transplanting. Top-dress Urea at 21 & 42 DAT. Zinc sulphate 25kg/ha if deficiency.
Paddy.disease: Watch for Blast (grey lesions), BLB (yellow leaf margins), Brown Spot. Spray Tricyclazole 0.6g/L for Blast; Copper Oxychloride for BLB.
Paddy.pest: Stem borer (dead hearts), BPH (hopper burn), Leaf folder. Chlorpyrifos 20EC 2ml/L for stem borer. Drain field to control BPH.
Paddy.harvest: Harvest at 80–85% grain maturity. Dry to 14% moisture for safe storage.
Paddy.market: Paddy MSP ₹2,300/qt (Common). Kerala procurement via SUPPLYCO/SFC. Best price: Nov–Jan.
Paddy.season: Kharif (Virippu): Jun–Nov. Rabi (Mundakan): Nov–Mar. Summer (Puncha): Jan–May.

Rice.irrigate: Same as Paddy — 2–5cm standing water. Critical periods: transplanting, tillering, flowering.
Rice.fertilize: NPK 90:45:45 kg/ha split 3 times. Zinc sulphate 25kg/ha basal if deficient.
Rice.disease: Blast, BLB, Sheath Blight. Tricyclazole for Blast; Validamycin 3ml/L for Sheath Blight.
Rice.pest: Yellow stem borer, BPH. Use pheromone traps. Drain field for BPH control.
Rice.harvest: 20–25% grain moisture at harvest. Use combine or power thresher.
Rice.market: Paddy MSP ₹2,300/qt. Kuttanad rice (Alappuzha) premium market.

Coconut.irrigate: 200L/palm every 4–7 days in summer. Drip: 25L/day/palm. Critical: Feb–May. Skip if >30mm/week rain.
Coconut.fertilize: 1.3kg N + 0.9kg P + 3.4kg K per palm/year. Split May–Jun and Sep–Oct. Add FYM 25kg/palm.
Coconut.disease: Bud Rot: Metalaxyl 1g/L drench. Root Wilt (phytoplasma): remove infected palms. Stem Bleeding: Bordeaux paste.
Coconut.pest: Rhinoceros Beetle: pheromone traps + Metarhizium. Red Palm Weevil: ethyl acetate trap. Eriophyid mite: sulfur dust.
Coconut.harvest: Harvest mature nuts every 45 days. Green nuts for tender coconut: 7–8 months. Target 100+ nuts/palm/year.
Coconut.market: Coconut ₹32–38 each. Copra ₹110–130/kg. Best price: Mar–May.
Coconut.season: Perennial. Peak harvest: Jan–Apr. Planting: Jun–Oct.

Rubber.irrigate: Rain-fed. Supplement only in severe drought (>30 dry days). Young trees: 15L/tree twice weekly.
Rubber.fertilize: N:P:K 10:10:4 + Mg 1.5 kg/tree/yr. Two splits: May–Jun and Sep–Oct.
Rubber.disease: Abnormal Leaf Fall: Aliette 3g/L before rains. Corynespora: Mancozeb 2.5g/L. Oidium: sulfur dust.
Rubber.pest: Mealybug: dimethoate 1.5ml/L. Scale insects: white oil emulsion. Bird's eye spot: copper fungicide.
Rubber.harvest: Tapping season Nov–Jun. Alternate day tapping. Target 1,500–2,000 kg/ha/yr.
Rubber.market: RSS4 ₹160–190/kg. KRRDB procurement. Best prices: Dec–Mar.
Rubber.season: Tapping: Nov–Jun. Rest (wintering): Jul–Aug. New planting: Jun–Jul.

Pepper.irrigate: Drip: 8–10L/vine/day in summer. Critical: Feb–May. Avoid waterlogging at all times.
Pepper.fertilize: NPK 50:50:150 g/vine/yr + FYM 10kg. Two splits: May & Sep. MgSO4 100g/vine for yellowing.
Pepper.disease: Phytophthora/Quick Wilt: Potassium Phosphonate 3ml/L. Anthracnose: Copper Oxychloride 2.5g/L.
Pepper.pest: Pollu beetle: pheromone traps + Carbaryl 3g/L. Pepper weevil: endosulfan 2ml/L.
Pepper.harvest: Harvest when 1–2 berries/spike turn red. Dry to 10% moisture. 3kg green → 1kg dry.
Pepper.market: Black Pepper ₹520–580/kg (Kochi). Best export quality: Nov–Feb harvest.
Pepper.season: Flowering: Feb–Mar. Harvest: Nov–Feb. Planting: Jun–Jul.

Banana.irrigate: 25–30L/plant/day via drip. Critical: bunch development. Stop 2 weeks before harvest.
Banana.fertilize: NPK 200:60:300 g/plant + FYM 10kg in 3 splits. Extra K for Nendran finger filling.
Banana.disease: Panama Wilt (Fusarium): remove & destroy — no cure. Sigatoka: Propiconazole 1ml/L. Bunchy Top: rogue infected plants.
Banana.pest: Corm weevil: chlorpyrifos at planting. Pseudostem borer: inject Monocrotophos. Thrips: imidacloprid.
Banana.harvest: Harvest when fingers are 3/4 round. Nendran: 12–14 months. Robusta: 9–10 months.
Banana.market: Nendran ₹35–55/kg retail. Best price: Onam season (Aug–Sep).
Banana.season: Plant Jun–Jul. Harvest ~12 months for Nendran.

Tapioca.irrigate: Drought tolerant. Supplement only in severe dry spells: 30L/plant every 10 days.
Tapioca.fertilize: NPK 100:50:100 kg/ha. Basal K at planting, top-dress N at 2 months. MOP critical for starch.
Tapioca.disease: Mosaic Virus (CMD): use virus-free stakes only. Bacterial Blight: remove infected.
Tapioca.pest: Whitefly: yellow sticky traps + imidacloprid. Red spider mite: sulfur dust.
Tapioca.harvest: 8–12 months. Fresh market: 7–8 months. Processing: 10–12 months. Yield: 25–35 t/ha.
Tapioca.market: Fresh ₹15–25/kg farm gate. Starch ₹28–35/kg. Thiruvananthapuram & Kollam main markets.
Tapioca.season: Plant Jun–Jul with monsoon onset. Also Oct–Nov for summer harvest.

Cardamom.irrigate: Shade + moisture critical. Drip: 8L/plant/day. Maintain mulch to conserve soil moisture.
Cardamom.fertilize: NPK 75:75:150 g/plant/yr + FYM 5kg. Apply Jun and Sep.
Cardamom.disease: Katte Virus (Mosaic): no cure — remove infected clumps. Capsule rot (Phytophthora): Copper Oxychloride.
Cardamom.pest: Thrips: spray thiamethoxam. Cardamom shoot borer: chlorpyrifos.
Cardamom.harvest: Harvest capsules when 3/4 mature (green). Dry in curing chambers to 12% moisture.
Cardamom.market: Cardamom ₹1,400–1,600/kg (Kumily). Premium grade exports through SPICE BOARD.
Cardamom.season: Flowering: Mar–May. Harvest: Aug–Jan. Replanting: Jun–Jul.

Coffee.irrigate: Rain-fed but supplement in Feb–Apr (flowering). 15L/tree every 5 days during dry spell.
Coffee.fertilize: NPK 40:20:40 g/plant/yr for Robusta. Apply May and Sep. Borax spray 0.2% at flowering.
Coffee.disease: White Stem Borer: inject endosulfan in stem holes. Berry Borer: pheromone traps. Leaf Rust: Copper fungicide.
Coffee.pest: Mealy bug: dimethoate spray. Nematodes: soil treatment with carbofuran.
Coffee.harvest: Robusta: Nov–Jan. Arabica: Dec–Feb. Pulp immediately or dry as parchment.
Coffee.market: Robusta ₹165–195/kg (Wayanad). Arabica premium ₹250–300/kg. Coffee Board procurement.
Coffee.season: Flowering: Feb–Mar after pre-monsoon showers. Harvest: Nov–Feb.

Tea.irrigate: Well-distributed rainfall ideal. Supplement if >15 dry days: sprinkler irrigation essential.
Tea.fertilize: High N demand: 150–180 kg N/ha/yr in 4 splits. Pruning years: reduce by 30%.
Tea.disease: Blister Blight: copper spray. Die-back: prune infected stems. Red spider mite: miticide.
Tea.pest: Tea mosquito bug: carbaryl spray. Looper caterpillar: endosulfan.
Tea.harvest: Pluck 2 leaves + bud every 7–10 days in active season. Quality: young shoots only.
Tea.season: Active flush: Mar–Nov. Pruning: Nov–Dec. Light pruning alternate years.

Ginger.irrigate: Well-drained soils essential. 35–40mm/week. Avoid waterlogging — causes rhizome rot.
Ginger.fertilize: NPK 75:50:50 kg/ha + FYM 30t/ha. Top-dress N at 45 & 90 days.
Ginger.disease: Rhizome Rot (Pythium): Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 2.5g/L soil drench. Bacterial Wilt: no chemical control — rogue.
Ginger.pest: Shoot borer: chlorpyrifos 2ml/L. Red spider mite: sulfur dust. White grubs: soil treatment.
Ginger.harvest: 8–9 months. Fresh ginger: 6 months. Dry ginger: 8–9 months. Yield: 15–25 t/ha.
Ginger.market: Fresh ginger ₹35–60/kg. Dry ginger ₹120–180/kg. Kozhikode & Ernakulam main markets.

Vegetables.irrigate: Drip/sprinkler: 25–30mm/week. Sandy soils: every 2 days. Clay: every 4–5 days.
Vegetables.fertilize: Leafy: NPK 60:30:30. Fruiting: NPK 60:60:60 + Ca for blossom-end rot. Organic mulching reduces need by 30%.
Vegetables.disease: Downy Mildew: Mancozeb 2.5g/L. Fusarium Wilt: Carbendazim drench. Rotate crops every season.
Vegetables.pest: Fruit borer: pheromone traps + Spinosad 0.5ml/L. Aphids: soap solution or imidacloprid.
Vegetables.harvest: Harvest at physiological maturity. Early morning harvest. Pre-cool before market.
Vegetables.market: Peak prices: Mar–May (summer scarcity). VFPCK procurement across Kerala.
Vegetables.season: Kharif: Jun–Sep. Rabi (best): Oct–Feb. Summer: Mar–May (irrigation-intensive).

Cashew.irrigate: Drought tolerant. Young trees: 30L/tree twice weekly in summer. Mature: rain-fed.
Cashew.fertilize: NPK 500:125:125 g/tree/yr. Two splits: Sep–Oct and Jan–Feb. Borax spray 0.2% at flowering.
Cashew.disease: Die-back: prune, copper paste. Powdery Mildew: wettable sulfur during flowering. Anthracnose: Copper Oxychloride.
Cashew.pest: Tea Mosquito Bug (key pest): Carbaryl 2g/L during flushing. Stem/Root borer: chlorpyrifos.
Cashew.harvest: Fallen nuts + apple: May–Jun. Kernel recovery 22–25%. Dry nuts to <9% moisture.
Cashew.market: Raw cashew ₹120–180/kg. Kernels (W320) ₹800–1,200/kg. Kollam is cashew capital.
Cashew.season: Flowering: Dec–Feb. Harvest: Mar–May. Prune: Jun–Jul.

Spices.irrigate: Varies by spice. Most need well-drained moist soils. Drip irrigation preferred.
Spices.fertilize: Follow individual crop schedules. Generally: high K for quality. Organic inputs improve flavour.
Spices.disease: Fungal diseases dominant in monsoon. Copper-based fungicides as preventive.
Spices.pest: Pollu beetle (pepper), thrips (cardamom), mealy bug (vanilla). Pheromone traps recommended.
Spices.harvest: Harvest at right maturity for maximum essential oil/flavour content.
Spices.market: Kerala spices command premium prices. Kochi Spice Exchange for black pepper, cardamom.
Spices.season: Most spices: harvest Oct–Feb after monsoon.

Arecanut.irrigate: Drip: 15L/palm/day in summer. Basin: 100L every 5 days. Critical: Feb–Apr.
Arecanut.fertilize: NPK 100:40:140 g/palm/yr + FYM 12kg. Two splits: Jun and Sep.
Arecanut.disease: Mahali (Phytophthora): Metalaxyl drench. Koleroga (fruit rot): Copper Oxychloride spray during monsoon.
Arecanut.pest: Mites: sulfur dust. Mealy bug: dimethoate. Spindle bug: chlorpyrifos.
Arecanut.harvest: Harvest every 40 days. Soft arecanut (chali): immature stage. Dry: fully mature & dried.
Arecanut.market: Arecanut ₹350–450/kg (whole). Kannur & Kasaragod main trading centres.

Coir.irrigate: Coir is processed coconut husk — no irrigation needed.
Coir.fertilize: N/A — processing industry, not direct cultivation.
Coir.harvest: Retting of coconut husks: 6–10 months in backwaters. Mechanical retting 3–4 days.
Coir.market: Coir yarn ₹60–90/kg. Coir geotextiles export premium. Alappuzha main coir hub.
Coir.season: Year-round processing. Peak: Jan–Apr (dry season for sun-drying).

Jackfruit.irrigate: Young trees require regular watering during summer. Mature trees are mostly rain-fed but benefit from irrigation during prolonged dry spells. Avoid waterlogging.
Jackfruit.fertilize: Apply FYM 25–30kg/tree annually. Recommended NPK application in split doses before and after monsoon. Mulching helps conserve moisture.
Jackfruit.disease: Common diseases include fruit rot and pink disease. Remove affected branches and apply copper-based fungicides when necessary.
Jackfruit.pest: Fruit borer, shoot borer and bark-eating caterpillars are major pests. Maintain orchard sanitation and use recommended control measures.
Jackfruit.harvest: Fruits are ready when the spines become widely spaced and produce a dull hollow sound on tapping. Harvest carefully to avoid fruit damage.
Jackfruit.market: Jackfruit has strong demand for fresh consumption and value-added products. Peak demand occurs during the main harvest season.
Jackfruit.season: Flowering generally occurs from December to February. Main harvesting season in Kerala is March to June.

Nutmeg.irrigate: Maintain adequate soil moisture, especially during summer. Young plants require regular irrigation, while mature trees benefit from watering during prolonged dry periods. Avoid water stagnation.
Nutmeg.fertilize: Apply FYM 10–15kg/tree annually along with recommended NPK fertilizers in split doses before and after monsoon. Mulching helps improve soil fertility and moisture retention.
Nutmeg.disease: Common diseases include fruit rot, leaf spot and die-back. Remove affected plant parts and apply recommended copper-based fungicides when necessary.
Nutmeg.pest: Scale insects, mealybugs and stem borers are important pests. Regular monitoring, orchard sanitation and recommended control measures help reduce infestation.
Nutmeg.harvest: Nutmeg fruits are harvested when they split open naturally, exposing the seed covered by mace. Dry seeds and mace separately before storage.
Nutmeg.market: Nutmeg and mace are high-value spice products with strong domestic and export demand. Quality-dried produce fetches premium prices.
Nutmeg.season: Flowering generally occurs during the rainy season. Harvesting is usually spread over multiple months, with peak harvest often from June to August in Kerala
